mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate held near the zero lower bound (FEDFUNDS at 0.09%) compresses the equity risk premium discount rate structurally, supporting a persistent upward drift in S&P 500 prices over quarterly horizons as investors reach for yield in equities versus near-zero cash returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or SPX 60-day returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) during periods when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid decline in the unemployment rate of more than 0.5 percentage points over two consecutive monthly readings signals accelerating labor market recovery, which raises consensus earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls by more than 0.5pp over trailing 2-month window
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE fell from double-digit peak toward 7.9% in 2020 recovery
failure_condition: In more than 40% of historical instances where UNRATE dropped >0.5pp over two months, 20-day forward SPX returns were negative, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) and nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals a self-reinforcing consumption-employment feedback loop that is not captured by either series alone; simultaneous positive month-over-month growth in both indicates broadening economic recovery and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.5% AND PAYEMS month-over-month growth > 0.3% concurrently
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with synchronized demand and labor rebound
failure_condition: Joint-trigger instances produce 20-day forward positive returns in less than 55% of cases, or the interaction term shows no incremental predictive power (Rank IC) over either series modeled independently.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) rebounding from a pandemic-induced trough signals reopening of supply chains and factory capacity utilization, which in a post-shock recovery regime drives positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon as cyclical sectors re-rate upward.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 101.36 in Aug 2020 recovering from 2020Q2 trough
failure_condition: During periods of INDPRO month-over-month increase in a recognized post-shock recovery regime, 60-day forward SPX returns are negative in more than 45% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) surging above 1400 thousand units signals robust consumer confidence and low mortgage rate transmission, which historically leads broader risk-asset appreciation as housing wealth effects and construction employment support aggregate demand over the following month.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1416.0 thousand units as of Aug 2020
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns following HOUST readings above 1400k are not significantly different from unconditional baseline returns (t-test p > 0.10) over the sample period.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden acceleration in the CPI index above trend signals emerging inflation pressure that raises expectations of future policy tightening, compressing valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over a short one-week horizon as markets price in tighter future liquidity conditions.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns following CPI acceleration triggers are positive in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained multi-month uptrend in total nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects durable labor market strengthening that supports consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, generating a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 prices over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 141720 thousand persons as of Sep 2020, recovering from 2020 trough
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC between trailing 3-month PAYEMS change and 60-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.03, or positive PAYEMS trend periods do not outperform negative-trend periods on average return (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production recovery combined with near-zero federal funds rate creates a reinforcing regime where cheap financing amplifies the equity market response to improving factory output; the interaction of accelerating INDPRO with ultra-low FEDFUNDS produces sharper short-term positive S&P 500 reactions than either signal alone during accommodative monetary regimes.
regime_hint: zero-rate accommodative monetary regime during industrial recovery
failure_condition: Joint-trigger 5-day forward SPX returns are not significantly higher than the average of INDPRO-only or FEDFUNDS-only single-factor conditional returns (p > 0.10), indicating no true interaction effect.
